name: climate
label: Climate Science & Policy — Knowledge Base
description: Climate change organizations, agreements, leading scientists, technologies and national policies.
observations: The IPCC is the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change established in 1988
The IPCC is a body of the United Nations and the World Meteorological Organization
The IPCC Sixth Assessment Report published in 2021 warned of irreversible climate tipping points
Katharine Hayhoe is a leading climate scientist and professor at Texas Tech University
Katharine Hayhoe focuses on communicating climate science to non-scientific audiences
Michael Mann is a climate scientist and professor at the University of Pennsylvania
Michael Mann co-created the hockey stick graph showing rapid modern temperature rise
The Paris Agreement was adopted in 2015 by 196 countries at COP21
The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels
Germany committed to achieving carbon neutrality by 2045 under its Climate Action Plan
Germany generates over 50 percent of its electricity from renewable sources
Denmark produces more than 60 percent of its electricity from wind power
Denmark set a binding target to reduce emissions by 70 percent by 2030
Carbon capture and storage is a technology that removes CO2 directly from industrial emissions
CarbonBridge is a climate tech startup developing direct air carbon capture facilities
CarbonBridge was founded in 2019 and raised 200 million dollars in Series B funding
CarbonBridge operates its first commercial plant in Reykjavik Iceland
Solar energy capacity worldwide exceeded 1 terawatt for the first time in 2023
The cost of utility-scale solar energy has fallen by 90 percent since 2010
The IPCC AR6 report identified fossil fuel combustion as the dominant cause of warming
The Global Carbon Project publishes annual estimates of global CO2 emissions
Global CO2 emissions reached 36.8 gigatons in 2023 according to the Global Carbon Project
Katharine Hayhoe advises the US State Department on climate communication strategy
Michael Mann is the author of the book The New Climate War published in 2021
The Paris Agreement created a framework for nationally determined contributions updated every 5 years
Germany invested 250 billion euros in its Energiewende energy transition program
Denmark's Orsted company is the world's largest offshore wind energy developer
CarbonBridge technology captures one ton of CO2 for under 300 dollars as of 2024
The IPCC is composed of thousands of volunteer scientists from over 130 countries
Solar and wind together accounted for 30 percent of global electricity generation in 2024
test_queries: What is the IPCC and what did their most recent major report conclude?
What targets has Germany set for climate and how are they funding the transition?
Who are the leading climate scientists and what are they known for?
What is CarbonBridge and how does it address climate change?
What does the Paris Agreement require and how often are commitments updated?
